compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
A Jira issue created directly in Jira (no rebar binding) is never adopted into the local store at steady state. Proven: REB-532 ("EXPERIMENT (delete me): assignee clobber repro", Bug, To Do) is fetched every pass but has no binding and no local ticket.

## Root Cause (edge-triggered adoption + prev_snapshot as a false "handled" proxy)
- REB-532 IS fetched — the inbound JQL has no rebar-label filter (`fetcher.py:67-79`).
- The binding-aware `inbound_differ.compute_inbound_mutations` skips any unbound issue by design (`inbound_differ.py:719-722`, docstring `:4-6` "local is source of truth"); it only emits `update`, never `create`.
- Inbound-CREATE lives only in `differ.compute_mutations`, which production calls as `compute_mutations(prev_snapshot, curr_snapshot)` — BOTH Jira snapshots (`reconcile.py:901-903`). Create fires only for a key in `curr` but NOT `prev` (`differ.py:457-469`) = "new since last pass".
- `prev_snapshot` means "seen in a prior fetch", used as a proxy for "already handled" — but it is NOT "bound to a local ticket". `reconcile.py:1407` advances prev via `shutil.copy2(curr, prev)` UNCONDITIONALLY, regardless of whether adoption actually landed. So once REB-532 is in prev it is in both snapshots -> present-in-both (field-drift) branch -> never re-adopted. There is NO level-triggered reconvergence that asks "fetched key has no binding -> adopt".
- Runtime proof: `compute_mutations(prev={}, curr={REB-532}) -> 1 inbound create`; `compute_mutations(prev={REB-532}, curr={REB-532}) -> 0`.

## Why the test suite misses it
- The differ unit tests model `compute_mutations(arg1, arg2)` as `(local_state, jira_state)` (`test_differ.py:1-13`), so an unbound issue is always absent from arg1 and create always fires — validating a contract production does NOT use.
- Every adoption test uses `local={}` (empty prev) incl. the closest-named `test_unbound_jira_issue_without_rebar_id_label_still_creates` (`test_differ.py:542`).
- The only two-pass integration test binds everything in pass 1 (`test_reconcile_idempotency.py:268,291`), so the "in prev but still unbound" state is never constructed; the unconditional `copy2` at `reconcile.py:1407` is untested.
- `inbound_differ` tests assert unbound issues are ignored (`test_inbound_differ.py:112`), entrenching the skip.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Create an issue directly in Jira REB (no rebar-id), e.g. REB-532.
2. Let >= 2 reconcile passes run (so it enters prev_snapshot).
3. Run `rebar reconcile --mode dry-run` and search the plan for the issue.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: a Jira-native issue is adopted into the local store (a `jira-*` local ticket + confirmed binding) and stays converged.
- Actual: it is fetched but never adopted; permanently orphaned in Jira.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A Jira-native issue with no binding is adopted into local within one pass, regardless of whether it is already in `prev_snapshot` (level-triggered, not just edge-triggered) — without re-deriving all historical creates every pass (respect the prev_snapshot perf optimisation).
- [ ] `prev_snapshot` advance (`reconcile.py:1407`) no longer marks an issue "handled" when adoption did not actually bind it (decouple prev-advance from adoption success), OR a binding-aware reconvergence adopts unbound fetched issues.
- [ ] A RED test constructs the failing state: the SAME key present in both `prev_snapshot` and `curr_snapshot` with NO binding, then asserts it is adopted.
- [ ] REB-532 is adopted (or explicitly excluded as a test artifact) after the fix.

## Notes
Discovered comparing Jira REB vs local. Files: `differ.py`, `inbound_differ.py`, `reconcile.py`, `fetcher.py`, `apply_inbound.py`; tests under `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/`, `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/`.

## Guardrails (must-preserve — prior-art + archaeology; folds in the corrected plan)
Approved policy: ADOPT unbound Jira-native issues (test-ticket hygiene is the creator's responsibility). But adoption is gated:
- [ ] Adoption MUST consult `bindings-retired.json` (`is_retired`) so a locally-archived-then-retired issue is not resurrected into a delete/re-adopt loop (interacts with C's GC).
- [ ] Run the rebar-id label audit (L9) and the label-derived-binding suppression (L10, bug 4354) FIRST: an issue already carrying a `rebar-id:`/`rebar-id-` label is bound — adopting it double-writes identity and recreates the phantom `jira-dig-NNNN` + ghost-label defect and can trip the double-bind quarantine (L11).
- [ ] Seed the per-binding BASELINE from the adopted issue's fields on bind, so the first outbound diff is EMPTY (echo suppression, L2).
- [ ] Adoption `create + bind` is keyed on the external id (idempotency/dedup key) so two overlapping passes cannot create duplicate local items before the binding lands [prior art: K8s read-all-then-write].
- [ ] Level-triggered: `unbound = fetched_keys - bound_keys` each pass (replaces the `prev_snapshot` edge-trigger), so a failed adoption re-fires until it binds. Do NOT rely on `prev_snapshot` for the trigger.
- [ ] Proven by the convergence property with an issue present in BOTH prev/curr snapshots and unbound (the exact steady-state cell the current suite never constructs).

## Implementation contract (foundation-first — read before coding)
DEPENDS ON amiss-bun-wince (baf7): this bug is the `ADOPT` / `SKIP_RETIRED` Decision cells of `classify()` plus the gated adopt handler. The trigger is LEVEL-based: `unbound = fetched_keys - bound_jira_keys` computed EVERY pass from the binding store — prev_snapshot plays NO role in the trigger (its unconditional advance at pass end stays as-is for now; the rollout ticket owns its future).
Adoption handler ORDER (ADR 0027 #4 — each step has an existing helper; do not reimplement):
1. `BindingStore.is_retired(jira_key)` -> Decision SKIP_RETIRED (prevents the delete/re-adopt loop with the GC bug 13eb).
2. rebar-id label audit (rebar_id_audit.py, L9) + label-derived-binding suppression (differ.py L10 region): an issue already carrying rebar-id:/rebar-id- is BOUND — route to binding repair, never double-create (the phantom jira-dig-NNNN defect).
3. Create local ticket + bind keyed on the EXTERNAL id (idempotency: a second overlapping pass must no-op); use the existing bind_pending -> create -> marker -> bind_confirm write-ahead protocol (binding_store.py docstring).
4. Immediately `set_baseline(local_id, adopted_fields)` (foundation method) so the FIRST outbound diff is empty — echo suppression per ADR 0027 #4c / ADR 0029 #3.
RED test (the exact cell today's suite never constructs): SAME key in prev_snapshot AND curr_snapshot, no binding -> assert ADOPT fires. Plus: retired key -> SKIP_RETIRED; labeled key -> no double-bind (L11 quarantine NOT tripped).
Live acceptance cell: REB-532 adopted (or explicitly archived in Jira as a test artifact — decide and record in a closing comment).
Files: classify.py (cells), apply_inbound.py or a new adopt handler beside dispatch_one.py, binding_store.py (consume, don't modify), fetcher.py (no changes — JQL already fetches unbound), tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/classify/ + diffing/ + integration two-pass test.
compiled_state.title: Reconciler never adopts a Jira-native issue at steady state (edge-triggered inbound-create + prev_snapshot proxy)

verify_commands.command: rebar reconcile --mode dry-run  # plan contains adopt REB-532
